templates.title: Product Manager
templates.purpose: Own product prioritization, requirement clarity, user-flow reasoning, and roadmap tradeoffs for the project.
when_to_recommend: The main gap is product prioritization, scope decisions, or workflow clarity.
The founder needs a persistent owner for turning product goals into structured decisions and clear outputs.
User journeys, onboarding, or feature tradeoffs materially affect the project.
when_not_to_recommend: The project only needs implementation planning inside engineering.
The product is so undefined that initial founder discovery matters more than a persistent PM role.

templates.title: Product Marketer
templates.purpose: Own messaging, segmentation, launch framing, and value communication for the product.
when_to_recommend: The project needs clearer positioning, messaging, or launch communication.
The founder needs help translating product value into market language.
The current bottleneck is understanding, conversion narrative, or competitive framing.
when_not_to_recommend: The primary need is channel-specific execution only.
The product is too undefined for meaningful positioning work beyond initial discovery.

templates.title: Growth Lead
templates.purpose: Own experiment design and prioritization across acquisition, activation, monetization, and retention.
when_to_recommend: The main gap is growth diagnosis and experiment prioritization.
The project needs a persistent owner for growth loops and KPI tradeoffs.
The founder needs help deciding where to focus growth effort next.
when_not_to_recommend: The need is limited to one channel such as SEO or paid.
The project is too early to support a broad growth role and needs a narrower operator first.

templates.title: SEO Lead
templates.purpose: Own search discovery strategy, content architecture, and technical SEO priorities for organic acquisition.
when_to_recommend: Search intent and content discovery materially affect acquisition.
The project has or needs content pages, landing pages, or programmatic SEO surfaces.
External search patterns are important to understanding demand.
when_not_to_recommend: The project has little meaningful search surface.
Growth is driven almost entirely by partnerships, direct sales, or product-led loops unrelated to search.

templates.title: Community Lead
templates.purpose: Own community loops, audience engagement systems, and feedback-driven growth surfaces for the project.
when_to_recommend: Community or audience participation materially affects growth, retention, or insight collection.
The project needs a persistent owner for feedback loops, ambassador systems, or founder-led community programs.
The startup benefits from recurring interaction with builders, users, or advocates.
when_not_to_recommend: The product has no meaningful repeat audience interaction outside the product.
The main need is one-way content distribution rather than community systems.

templates.title: Content Strategist
templates.purpose: Own editorial strategy, narrative consistency, and content system design for the project.
when_to_recommend: The project needs recurring content output tied to growth, education, or authority.
A domain-aware editorial system matters more than one-off copywriting.
The team needs content planning beyond channel execution.
when_not_to_recommend: The need is only ad copy or launch copy.
Content is not a meaningful part of acquisition, retention, or positioning.

templates.title: CMO
templates.purpose: Own cross-channel marketing strategy, prioritization, and executive-level marketing decisions for the project.
when_to_recommend: Marketing strategy spans multiple channels or functions.
The founder needs a persistent owner for growth direction, positioning, and prioritization.
The project has enough marketing surface area that narrow specialists need orchestration.
when_not_to_recommend: The project only needs narrow channel execution.
A growth lead or product marketer would cover the actual gap more precisely.
The startup is still better served by a smaller role set with direct ownership.

templates.title: Lifecycle / CRM Lead
templates.purpose: Own activation, retention, re-engagement, and triggered messaging strategy.
when_to_recommend: Retention or activation is a core bottleneck.
The product has enough user lifecycle events to support segmented messaging.
The team needs a persistent owner for CRM experiments and retention hypotheses.
when_not_to_recommend: The product does not yet have meaningful repeat usage.
The project lacks the operational ability to execute lifecycle messaging in the near term.
